task_completion.description: Create the cross-section profile for the pipe
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch visible in the FreeCAD workspace showing two concentric circles representing the outer diameter (25mm) and inner diameter (21mm).

task_completion.description: Create the centerline path for the 90-degree bend
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch or drafted line visible in the FreeCAD workspace showing a 90-degree arc, representing the 40mm bend radius.
A 3D solid model of a 90-degree curved pipe visible in the FreeCAD 3D viewport. The tree view should show a sweep, pipe, or loft operation.

task_completion.description: Ensure the elbow is a single hollow solid
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ends of the 3D pipe model clearly show an annular (ring-like) face with an open center, confirming the structure is hollow and not a solid cylinder.

task_completion.description: Save the file with the correct name and path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'pipe_elbow.FCStd' is visible in the FreeCAD window title bar, the tree view root, or within a 'Save As' dialog targeting the /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeCAD GUI to create the model
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active use of FreeCAD workbenches (e.g., Part Design, Sketcher, Part), toolbars, and the Combo View.

integrity.description: The model was built using standard CAD operations rather than importing a pre-made file
integrity.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD tree view (Model tab) displays a history of operations such as Sketches, Datums, Sweeps, or Thickness features, rather than a single imported mesh or uneditable solid.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a terminal script to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or FreeCAD Python console was not used to execute a complete macro that instantly generates the file without GUI interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The pipe elbow must have an outer diameter of 25mm (radius 12.5mm) and an inner diameter of 21mm (radius 10.5mm, wall thickness 2mm). The bend must be exactly 90 degrees with a centerline radius of 40mm. The final model should be a single hollow solid with at least 4 faces (top/bottom annular faces, inner/outer curved surfaces). The expected bounding box dimensions are approximately 25.0 x 52.5 x 52.5 mm. The file must be saved exactly as 'pipe_elbow.FCStd' in the /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/ directory.
